client_like.h(3) Library Functions Manual client_like.h(3)

NAME

client_like.h - Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_client_like_config

Typedefs


typedef struct tcp_client tcp_client_t
typedef struct websocket_client websocket_client_t
typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)
typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Functions


tcp_client_t * session_client_like_get_tcp_client (void)
websocket_client_t * session_client_like_get_websocket_client (void)
void session_client_like_set_websocket_client (websocket_client_t *client)
terminal_fd_reader_t * session_client_like_get_stdin_reader (void)
asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const session_client_like_config_t *config)

Variables


bool(*)(void *) session_client_like_get_render_should_exit (void)

Detailed Description

Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

Provides a unified interface for running client-like modes that share common initialization patterns: media source selection, FPS probing, audio initialization, display creation, splash screen management, keepawake handling, and cleanup.

Each mode registers mode-specific callbacks to handle initialization, the main render loop, and keyboard input. The shared layer handles all the boilerplate.

Modes Supported

  • Mirror mode: Local webcam/media playback without networking
  • Client mode: Network client with per-connection initialization
  • Discovery mode: P2P mode with role negotiation

Shared Responsibilities

This layer automatically handles:

  • Terminal output management (stdout piping detection)
  • Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
  • Splash screen lifecycle and animation
  • Media source selection and FPS probing
  • Audio initialization and lifecycle
  • Display context creation and management
  • Render loop execution
  • Proper cleanup ordering (critical for PortAudio)

Mode-Specific Responsibilities

Mode files provide:

  • Main loop callback (run_fn) that calls session_render_loop() or custom loop
  • Optional network interrupt callback for socket shutdown
  • Optional custom exit condition for additional shutdown criteria
  • Keyboard handler for interactive controls
  • Terminal behavior preferences (newline on exit)

Memory and Lifecycle

All allocations (capture, display, audio) are owned and cleaned up by session_client_like_run(). Mode callbacks receive initialized, ready-to-use contexts and should not attempt to free them.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file client_like.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Configuration for session_client_like_run()

All media, display, audio, FPS, and snapshot settings are read automatically from GET_OPTION() inside the function. Callers only provide the fields below that differ per mode.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)

Mode-specific main loop callback

Called after all shared initialization is complete (capture opened, audio started, display ready, splash done). The mode runs its main loop here and returns when finished. The shared teardown runs after this returns.

Typical pattern:

// For mirror/discovery participant:
return session_render_loop(capture, display, exit_check_fn, NULL, NULL,


                           keyboard_handler, user_data);
// For client mode (protocol-driven):
while (!should_exit()) {


  // protocol loop...
}
return ASCIICHAT_OK;

Parameters

capture Initialized capture context. Never NULL.
display Initialized display context. Never NULL.
user_data User data passed through from config.run_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code to abort (cleanup still runs)

Definition at line 95 of file client_like.h.

typedef struct tcp_client tcp_client_t

Definition at line 60 of file client_like.h.

typedef struct websocket_client websocket_client_t

Definition at line 61 of file client_like.h.

Function Documentation

tcp_client_t * session_client_like_get_tcp_client (void )

Get the TCP client created by session_client_like_run() (if applicable).

Returns the TCP client created for direct TCP connections (non-WebSocket). Only valid after session_client_like_run() is called and during run_fn execution. May be NULL if WebSocket is being used instead.

Returns

Pointer to tcp_client_t, or NULL if not created or using WebSocket

Definition at line 58 of file client_like.c.

58                                                        {
59   return g_tcp_client;
60 }








websocket_client_t *
  session_client_like_get_websocket_client (void )


Get the WebSocket client created by
    session_client_like_run() (if applicable).




Returns the WebSocket client created for WebSocket connections.
    Only valid after session_client_like_run() is called and during
    run_fn execution. May be NULL if TCP is being used instead.




Returns


Pointer to websocket_client_t, or NULL if not
  created or using TCP


Definition at line 62 of file client_like.c.


62                                                                    {
63   return g_websocket_client;
64 }










asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const
  session_client_like_config_t * config)


Run a client-like mode with fully shared initialization and
    teardown




This function orchestrates the complete lifecycle of client-like
    modes:








Initialization
  (automatic from GET_OPTION())


Terminal:


    
  
  • Forces stderr when stdout is piped (terminal_should_force_stderr)
    • 
  
  • Disables terminal logging during rendering, re-enables on exit
    • 
  
  • Handles snapshot mode log visibility
    • 



Keepawake:


    
  
  • Validates --keepawake / --no-keepawake mutual exclusivity
    • 
  
  • Calls platform_enable_keepawake() unless --no-keepawake set
    • 
  
  • Disables keepawake on exit
    • 



Splash Screen:


    
  
  • Creates temporary display context
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_start() to show splash animation
    • 
  
  • Holds splash visible during media probing and audio init
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_done() when capture and display are ready
    • 



Media / Webcam
    (session_capture_ctx_t):



  
•

  
Selects source type from options (priority order):





  
 1.

  
--url → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE (FFmpeg handles http/rtsp/rtmp)

  
 2.

  
--file → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE; "-" →
      MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN

  
 3.

  
--test-pattern → MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST

  
 4.

  
(default) → MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM




    
  
  • FPS: uses GET_OPTION(fps) if > 0, otherwise probes with
      temporary media source
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp) as initial seek
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_loop) for file looping
    • 
  
  • Calls session_capture_create() to open webcam or media file
    • 
  
  • Destroys capture on exit
    • 



Audio (audio_context_t):


    
  
  • Skipped entirely for immediate snapshots (snapshot_mode &&
      snapshot_delay == 0)
    • 
  
  • Probes for audio via media_source_has_audio() on capture's
    source
    • 
  
  • Allocates and initializes audio_context_t when audio available
    • 
  
  • Links audio context to capture and media source
    • 
  
  • Sets playback_only based on audio_should_enable_microphone()
    • 
  
  • Disables jitter buffering for local file playback
    • 
  
  • Calls audio_start_duplex() after splash_intro_done()
    • 
  
  • Stops, destroys, and frees audio on exit
    • 



Display (session_display_ctx_t):


    
  
  • Calls session_display_create() with snapshot and color
    settings
    • 
  
  • Passes audio context if initialized
    • 
  
  • Destroys display on exit
    • 







Mode-Specific
  Loop


Calls config->run_fn(capture, display,
    config->run_user_data) once all initialization is complete. This is where
    each mode calls session_render_loop() or runs its own
    protocol-thread-driven loop.








Cleanup
  (always runs, even on error)


Cleanup sequence (order is critical):



  
1.

  
Re-enable terminal logging

  
2.

  
audio_terminate_portaudio_final() - Free PortAudio device resources
      FIRST

  
3.

  
audio_stop_duplex() + audio_destroy() + SAFE_FREE

  
4.

  
session_display_destroy()

  
5.

  
session_capture_destroy()

  
6.

  
webcam_destroy() - Free cached webcam images and test patterns

  
7.

  
session_log_buffer_destroy()

  
8.

  
platform_disable_keepawake()

  
9.

  
Print '
    

  




Parameters


Config Module Mode configuration.
  Must not be NULL. run_fn must not be NULL.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or the first error code from
  initialization or from run_fn. Cleanup always runs regardless of return
  value.


Definition at line 108 of file client_like.c.


108                                                                                       {
109   if (!config || !config->run_fn) {
110     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config or run_fn is NULL");
111   }
112 
113   // Store config globally for adapter callbacks
114   g_current_config = config;
115 
116   // Store the render should_exit adapter globally so mode-specific run_fn can access it
117   g_render_should_exit = display_should_exit_adapter;
118 
119   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
120 
121   // Keep track of what's been initialized for cleanup
122   session_capture_ctx_t *capture = NULL;
123   session_display_ctx_t *display = NULL;
124   audio_context_t *audio_ctx = NULL;
125   media_source_t *probe_source = NULL;
126   bool audio_available = false;
127 
128   // ============================================================================
129   // SETUP: Terminal and Logging
130   // ============================================================================
131 
132   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up terminal and logging");
133 
134   // Force stderr when stdout is piped (prevents ASCII corruption in output stream)
135   bool should_force_stderr = terminal_should_force_stderr();
136   log_debug("terminal_should_force_stderr()=%d", should_force_stderr);
137 
138   if (should_force_stderr) {
139     // Redirect logs to stderr to prevent corruption of stdout (for pipes)
140     // But keep terminal output enabled so we can see initialization errors
141     log_set_force_stderr(true);
142   }
143 
144   // Note: Force all logging to stderr if encoding to pipe to prevent corrupting binary video output
145   // (implemented via should_force_stderr check above)
146   const char *render_file_early = GET_OPTION(render_file);
147   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_RUN] render_file_early=%s (ptr=%p)",
148            render_file_early ? render_file_early : "(null)", (void *)render_file_early);
149   if (render_file_early && render_file_early[0] != '\0' &&
150       (strcmp(render_file_early, "-") == 0 || strcmp(render_file_early, "pipe:") == 0)) {
151     log_info("--render-file=- will output binary video to stdout, logging sent to stderr");
152   }
153 
154   // ============================================================================
155   // SETUP: Keepawake System
156   // ============================================================================
157 
158   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Validating keepawake options");
159 
160   // Validate mutual exclusivity
161   bool enable_ka = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
162   bool disable_ka = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
163   log_debug("enable_keepawake=%d, disable_keepawake=%d", enable_ka, disable_ka);
164 
165   if (enable_ka && disable_ka) {
166     result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
167     goto cleanup;
168   }
169 
170   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Enabling keepawake if needed");
171 
172   // Enable keepawake unless explicitly disabled
173   if (!disable_ka) {
174     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();
175   }
176 
177   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Keepawake setup complete");
178 
179   // ============================================================================
180   // SETUP: Display Context (before splash for unified TTY initialization)
181   // ============================================================================
182 
183   // Create display context early to share TTY initialization between splash and rendering
184   // This prevents double TTY init which was corrupting the terminal state
185   session_display_config_t display_config = {0};
186   display_config.snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
187   display_config.palette_type = GET_OPTION(palette_type);
188   display_config.custom_palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set) ? GET_OPTION(palette_custom) : NULL;
189   display_config.color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
190   display_config.should_exit_callback = display_should_exit_adapter;
191   display_config.callback_data = NULL;
192 
193   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Creating display context (before splash)");
194   display = session_display_create(&display_config);
195   if (!display) {
196     log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] session_display_create() returned NULL");
197     log_fatal("Failed to initialize display context");
198     result = ERROR_DISPLAY;
199     goto cleanup;
200   }
201   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Display context created, will use for splash");
202 
203   // Register global display context for signal handlers (Ctrl+C help screen closure)
204   // This makes the context available to signal handlers for the entire app lifetime
205   session_display_set_global_context(display);
206 
207   // ============================================================================
208   // SETUP: Splash Screen (using unified display context)
209   // ============================================================================
210 
211   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Starting splash with display context");
212 
213   // Use the display context we just created for splash animation
214   splash_intro_start(display);
215   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): splash_intro_start() returned");
216 
217   // Detect if we're using media vs webcam (needed for splash timing)
218   const char *media_url = GET_OPTION(media_url);
219   const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
220   bool has_media = (media_url && strlen(media_url) > 0) || (media_file && strlen(media_file) > 0);
221 
222   // Show splash briefly for webcam, but skip sleep for media (which takes time anyway)
223   if (!has_media && !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
224     platform_sleep_ms(250);
225   }
226   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): After splash sleep");
227 
228   // Restore stderr now that splash animation and post-splash logging are done
229   // This allows logs to appear on screen again after this point
230   splash_restore_stderr();
231 
232   // ============================================================================
233   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
234   // ============================================================================
235 
236   // ============================================================================
237   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
238   // ============================================================================
239 
240   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): About to disable terminal logging (snapshot=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode));
241   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
242     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Calling log_set_terminal_output(false)...");
243     log_set_terminal_output(false);
244     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): RETURNED from log_set_terminal_output(false)");
245   }
246   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Terminal logging disabled, starting media source setup");
247 
248   // ============================================================================
249   // SETUP: Media Source Selection and FPS Probing
250   // ============================================================================
251 
252   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Initializing capture config");
253   session_capture_config_t capture_config = {0};
254   capture_config.resize_for_network = false;
255   capture_config.should_exit_callback = capture_should_exit_adapter;
256   capture_config.callback_data = NULL;
257 
258   // Determine FPS explicitly set by user
259   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting FPS option");
260   int user_fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
261   bool fps_explicitly_set = user_fps > 0;
262   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): FPS=%d (explicitly_set=%d)", user_fps, fps_explicitly_set);
263 
264   // Select media source based on options (priority order)
265   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_url option");
266   const char *media_url_val = GET_OPTION(media_url);
267   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_file option");
268   const char *media_file_val = GET_OPTION(media_file);
269   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): media_url=%s, media_file=%s", media_url_val ? media_url_val : "(null)",
270             media_file_val ? media_file_val : "(null)");
271 
272   if (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) {
273     // Network URL takes priority
274     log_info("Using network URL: %s (webcam disabled)", media_url_val);
275     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
276     capture_config.path = media_url_val;
277     capture_config.loop = false; // Network URLs cannot be looped
278 
279     if (fps_explicitly_set) {
280       capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
281       log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
282     } else {
283       // Always probe FPS for HTTP URLs, even in snapshot mode
284       // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
285       log_debug("Probing FPS for HTTP URL");
286       probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_url_val);
287       if (probe_source) {
288         double url_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
289         log_info("Detected HTTP stream video FPS: %.1f", url_fps);
290         if (url_fps > 0.0) {
291           capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(url_fps + 0.5);
292         } else {
293           log_warn("FPS detection failed for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
294           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
295         }
296       } else {
297         log_warn("Failed to create probe source for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
298         capture_config.target_fps = 60;
299       }
300     }
301   } else if (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) {
302     // Local file or stdin
303     log_info("DEBUG: media_file_val='%s', strcmp result=%d", media_file_val, strcmp(media_file_val, "-"));
304     if (strcmp(media_file_val, "-") == 0) {
305       log_info("Using stdin for media streaming (webcam disabled)");
306       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN;
307       capture_config.path = NULL;
308       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
309       capture_config.loop = false;
310     } else {
311       log_info("Using media file: %s (webcam disabled)", media_file_val);
312       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
313       capture_config.path = media_file_val;
314       capture_config.loop = GET_OPTION(media_loop);
315 
316       if (fps_explicitly_set) {
317         capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
318         log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
319       } else {
320         // Always probe FPS for local files, even in snapshot mode
321         // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
322         log_debug("Probing FPS for local file");
323         probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_file_val);
324         if (probe_source) {
325           double file_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
326           log_info("Detected file video FPS: %.1f", file_fps);
327           if (file_fps > 0.0) {
328             capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(file_fps + 0.5);
329           } else {
330             log_warn("FPS detection failed, using default 60 FPS");
331             capture_config.target_fps = 60;
332           }
333         } else {
334           log_warn("Failed to create probe source for FPS detection, using default 60 FPS");
335           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
336         }
337       }
338     }
339   } else if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
340     // Test pattern
341     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Using test pattern");
342     log_info("Using test pattern");
343     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
344     capture_config.path = NULL;
345     capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
346     capture_config.loop = false;
347   } else {
348     // Default: Try webcam if stdout is a TTY, otherwise use test pattern
349     // (webcam + piped output often fails, test pattern is reliable fallback)
350     bool stdout_is_tty = terminal_is_stdout_tty();
351     if (stdout_is_tty) {
352       // Normal terminal mode: use webcam
353       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is TTY, using local webcam");
354       log_info("Using local webcam");
355       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM;
356       capture_config.path = NULL;
357       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
358       capture_config.loop = false;
359     } else {
360       // Piped output mode: use test pattern (webcam + pipes often fails)
361       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is piped, defaulting to test pattern for reliability");
362       log_info("Stdout is piped - using test pattern instead of webcam");
363       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
364       capture_config.path = NULL;
365       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
366       capture_config.loop = false;
367     }
368   }
369 
370   // Apply initial seek if specified
371   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_seek_timestamp option");
372   capture_config.initial_seek_timestamp = GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp);
373   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Media seek timestamp set");
374 
375   // Clean up probe source (don't reuse for actual capture)
376   if (probe_source) {
377     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Destroying probe source");
378     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
379     probe_source = NULL;
380     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Probe source destroyed");
381   } else {
382     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): No probe source to destroy");
383   }
384 
385   // Pass probed FPS to display context for correct render-file duration
386   if (capture_config.target_fps > 0) {
387     session_display_set_render_fps(display, capture_config.target_fps);
388     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Set display render FPS to %u for correct video encoding duration",
389               capture_config.target_fps);
390   }
391 
392   // ============================================================================
393   // SETUP: Network Transports (TCP/WebSocket)
394   // ============================================================================
395 
396   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up network transports");
397 
398   // Use network clients from config if provided (client mode), or skip for mirror mode
399   // Mirror mode passes NULL for both tcp_client and websocket_client
400   g_tcp_client = config->tcp_client;
401   g_websocket_client = config->websocket_client;
402 
403   // Determine the networking mode based on available indicators:
404   // - If config has a discovery object, it's discovery mode (will create network clients later)
405   // - If config already has tcp_client or websocket_client, it's network mode (client mode)
406   // - Otherwise, it's mirror mode (local-only capture)
407   // Mirror mode has a run_fn (mirror_run) but no network components
408   bool discovery_mode = (config->discovery != NULL);
409   bool network_mode = (g_tcp_client != NULL || g_websocket_client != NULL);
410   bool mirror_mode = (!discovery_mode && !network_mode);
411 
412   if (mirror_mode) {
413     log_debug("Mirror mode detected - will use local capture with media source");
414   } else if (discovery_mode) {
415     log_debug("Discovery mode detected - discovery session will manage networking");
416   } else if (network_mode) {
417     log_debug("Client/Network mode detected - will use network capture without local media source");
418   }
419 
420   // ============================================================================
421   // SETUP: Capture Context
422   // ============================================================================
423 
424   // Check for stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, render to video
425   // Only enable stdin render mode if:
426   // 1. render-file is "-" (output to stdout)
427   // 2. stdin is not a TTY
428   // 3. --height is explicitly specified (user must opt-in to stdin render mode)
429   // 4. NO media source is being used (not reading from file/URL/test-pattern)
430   // This prevents accidental activation when piping to other processes or using mirror mode
431   const char *render_file_opt = GET_OPTION(render_file);
432   int frame_height = GET_OPTION(height);
433   bool height_explicitly_set = (frame_height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT);
434   bool has_media_source = (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) ||
435                           (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) ||
436                           GET_OPTION(test_pattern);
437 
438   // Enable audio capture if render-file is active and media source exists
439   if (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0 && has_media_source) {
440     capture_config.enable_audio = true;
441     log_debug("Audio capture enabled for render-file encoding");
442   }
443 
444   bool stdin_render_mode = (render_file_opt && strcmp(render_file_opt, "-") == 0 &&
445                             !terminal_is_stdin_tty() && height_explicitly_set &&
446                             !has_media_source && !mirror_mode);
447 
448   if (stdin_render_mode) {
449     // Stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, output video to stdout
450     log_info("Stdin render mode enabled: reading ASCII frames from stdin, output to stdout");
451 
452     // Get frame dimensions (--height must be explicitly set to reach here)
453     int frame_width = GET_OPTION(width);
454 
455     // Validate stdin render mode constraints (--height is already validated above since height_explicitly_set is true)
456     bool width_was_explicitly_set = (frame_width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT);
457 
458     if (width_was_explicitly_set) {
459       result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Stdin render mode does not accept --width (auto-detected from frames).\n"
460                                       "Only specify: --height <rows>");
461       goto cleanup;
462     }
463 
464     asciichat_error_t stdin_err = terminal_fd_reader_create(STDIN_FILENO, frame_height, &g_stdin_reader);
465     if (stdin_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
466       log_fatal("Failed to initialize stdin frame reader: %s", asciichat_error_string(stdin_err));
467       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
468       goto cleanup;
469     }
470 
471     // For stdin render mode, create a minimal capture context for compatibility
472     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
473     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
474     if (!capture) {
475       log_fatal("Failed to initialize capture context for stdin rendering");
476       terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
477       g_stdin_reader = NULL;
478       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
479       goto cleanup;
480     }
481 
482     log_info("stdin render mode: reading %d-line frames from stdin, auto-detecting width", frame_height);
483   }
484 
485   // Choose capture type based on mode determined earlier:
486   // - Mirror mode: needs to capture local media (webcam, file, test pattern)
487   // - Network modes (client/discovery): receive frames from network, no local capture
488   bool is_network_mode = network_mode || discovery_mode;
489 
490   if (!stdin_render_mode && is_network_mode) {
491     // Network mode: create minimal capture context without media source
492     log_debug("Network mode detected - using network capture (no local media source)");
493     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
494     uint64_t net_cap_start = time_get_ns();
495     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
496     double net_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(net_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
497     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Network capture created in %.1f ms", net_cap_ms);
498     if (!capture) {
499       log_fatal("Failed to initialize network capture context");
500       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
501       goto cleanup;
502     }
503     if (fps > 0) {
504       log_debug("Network capture FPS set to %d from options", fps);
505     }
506   }
507 
508   // ============================================================================
509   // SETUP: Audio Context
510   // ============================================================================
511 
512   // Skip audio for immediate snapshots
513   bool should_init_audio = true;
514   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0) {
515     should_init_audio = false;
516     log_debug("Skipping audio initialization for immediate snapshot");
517   }
518 
519   // Probe for audio
520   if (should_init_audio && capture_config.type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE && capture_config.path) {
521     media_source_t *audio_probe_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
522     if (audio_probe_source && media_source_has_audio(audio_probe_source)) {
523       audio_available = true;
524 
525       // Allocate and initialize audio context
526       audio_ctx = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(audio_context_t), audio_context_t *);
527       if (audio_ctx) {
528         *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t){0};
529         if (audio_init(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
530           // Link audio to media source
531           media_source_t *media_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
532           audio_ctx->media_source = media_source;
533 
534           if (media_source) {
535             media_source_set_audio_context(media_source, audio_ctx);
536           }
537 
538           // Determine if microphone should be enabled
539           bool should_enable_mic = audio_should_enable_microphone(GET_OPTION(audio_source), audio_available);
540           audio_ctx->playback_only = !should_enable_mic;
541 
542           // Disable jitter buffering for file playback
543           if (audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
544             audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_enabled = false;
545             atomic_store_bool(&audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
546           }
547 
548           // Store in capture for keyboard handler access
549           session_capture_set_audio_context(capture, audio_ctx);
550 
551           log_debug("Audio context initialized");
552         } else {
553           log_warn("Failed to initialize audio context");
554           audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
555           SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
556           audio_ctx = NULL;
557           audio_available = false;
558         }
559       }
560     }
561   }
562 
563   // Pass stdin_reader to display if in stdin render mode
564   if (stdin_render_mode && g_stdin_reader) {
565     session_display_set_stdin_reader(display, g_stdin_reader);
566     log_debug("stdin_reader passed to display context");
567   }
568 
569   // ============================================================================
570   // SETUP: End Splash Screen
571   // ============================================================================
572   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] About to end splash screen");
573 
574   // The splash screen will display during initialization and exit when done.
575   // The initialization process (media probing, display setup, etc.) naturally
576   // takes time, keeping the splash visible. The splash is kept alive during
577   // connection attempts (TCP and WebSocket) to prevent a blank screen during
578   // network operations. splash_intro_done() is called only after a successful
579   // connection is established.
580 
581   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Splash will remain visible during connection attempts");
582 
583   // Exit early if shutdown was requested (e.g., user pressed Ctrl-C)
584   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
585     log_debug("[SETUP] Shutdown requested, exiting early");
586     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Ending splash due to early shutdown request");
587     splash_intro_done();
588     splash_wait_for_animation();
589     result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
590     goto cleanup;
591   }
592 
593   // Wait for splash animation to finish before proceeding to render loop
594   // (splash_anim thread uses the display, must not be disrupted before cleanup)
595   // But skip for immediate snapshots (snapshot-delay=0) which need to output first frame immediately
596   splash_intro_done();
597   if (!(GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0)) {
598     splash_wait_for_animation();
599   }
600 
601   // ============================================================================
602   // SETUP: Create Mirror Capture (after splash screen finishes)
603   // ============================================================================
604 
605   log_info("[MIRROR_CHECK] stdin_render_mode=%d, capture=%p", stdin_render_mode, (void *)capture);
606   if (!stdin_render_mode && !capture) {
607     // Mirror mode: create capture context with local media source (NOW, after splash finishes)
608     log_info("Mirror mode detected - creating mirror capture after splash screen");
609     uint64_t mirror_cap_start = time_get_ns();
610     capture = session_mirror_capture_create(&capture_config);
611     double mirror_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(mirror_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
612     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Mirror capture created in %.1f ms (after splash)", mirror_cap_ms);
613     if (!capture) {
614       log_fatal("Failed to initialize mirror capture source");
615       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
616       goto cleanup;
617     }
618 
619     // Set audio source for render-file encoding (after capture is created)
620     media_source_t *render_audio_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
621     const char *render_file_path = GET_OPTION(render_file);
622     log_info("[AUDIO_SOURCE] render_audio_source=%p, render_file=%s, render_file_len=%zu",
623              render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
624              render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
625     if (render_audio_source && render_file_path && strlen(render_file_path) > 0) {
626       session_display_set_render_audio_source(display, render_audio_source);
627       log_info("Audio source set for render-file output");
628     } else {
629       log_warn("[AUDIO_SOURCE] Conditions not met: render_audio_source=%p, render_file_path=%s, len=%zu",
630                render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
631                render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
632     }
633   }
634 
635   // ============================================================================
636   // SETUP: Start Audio Playback
637   // ============================================================================
638 
639   log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] About to check audio context for duplex");
640   if (audio_ctx && audio_available) {
641     log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] Starting audio duplex");
642     if (audio_start_duplex(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
643       log_info("Audio playback started");
644     } else {
645       log_warn("Failed to start audio duplex");
646       audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
647       SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
648       audio_ctx = NULL;
649       audio_available = false;
650     }
651   }
652 
653   log_info("[MARKER] Audio setup complete at line 614");
654   log_info("[SETUP_COMPLETE] All setup complete, about to start connection loop");
655 
656   // ============================================================================
657   // RUN: Mode-Specific Main Loop with Reconnection
658   // ============================================================================
659 
660   // Connection/attempt loop - wraps run_fn with reconnection logic
661   int attempt = 0;
662   int max_attempts = config->max_reconnect_attempts;
663 
664   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_LOOP] Starting connection loop, max_attempts=%d", max_attempts);
665 
666   while (true) {
667     attempt++;
668 
669     result = config->run_fn(capture, display, config->run_user_data);
670 
671     // Exit immediately if run_fn succeeded
672     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
673       // Connection succeeded - splash screen cleanup now happens when first frame renders
674       // This ensures the splash stays visible during TCP/WebSocket/datachannel connection attempts
675 
676       break;
677     }
678 
679     // Check if we should attempt reconnection
680     bool should_retry = false;
681 
682     if (max_attempts != 0) { // max_attempts == 0 means no retries
683       // Check custom reconnect logic if provided
684       if (config->should_reconnect_callback) {
685         should_retry = config->should_reconnect_callback(result, attempt, config->reconnect_user_data);
686       } else {
687         should_retry = true; // Default: always retry
688       }
689 
690       // Check retry limits if still planning to retry
691       if (should_retry && max_attempts > 0 && attempt >= max_attempts) {
692         log_debug("Reached maximum reconnection attempts (%d), giving up", max_attempts);
693         should_retry = false;
694       }
695     }
696 
697     // If not retrying or shutdown requested, exit loop
698     if (!should_retry || APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
699       break;
700     }
701 
702     // Reconnection will happen - show splash screen during reconnection attempt
703     // Reset first_frame flag so splash cleanup runs on next successful connection
704     session_display_reset_first_frame(display);
705 
706     // splash_intro_start() handles all the logic:
707     // - If splash should display: shows splash and disables console logging
708     // - If splash shouldn't display: restores stderr and returns without changing logging state
709     splash_intro_start(display);
710 
711     // Log reconnection message
712     if (max_attempts == -1) {
713       log_info("Connection failed, retrying...");
714     } else if (max_attempts > 0) {
715       log_info("Connection failed, retrying (attempt %d/%d)...", attempt + 1, max_attempts);
716     }
717 
718     // Apply reconnection delay if configured
719     // Check APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit) frequently during sleep so SIGTERM can interrupt reconnection attempts
720     if (config->reconnect_delay_ms > 0) {
721       unsigned int remaining_ms = config->reconnect_delay_ms;
722       const unsigned int check_interval_ms = 100; // Check exit flag every 100ms
723 
724       while (remaining_ms > 0 && !APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
725         unsigned int sleep_ms = (remaining_ms < check_interval_ms) ? remaining_ms : check_interval_ms;
726         platform_sleep_ms(sleep_ms);
727         remaining_ms -= sleep_ms;
728       }
729     }
730 
731     // Continue loop to retry
732   }
733 
734   // ============================================================================
735   // CLEANUP (always runs, even on error)
736   // ============================================================================
737 
738 cleanup:
739   // Re-enable terminal output for shutdown logs
740   log_set_terminal_output(true);
741 
742   // Cleanup network transports (TCP/WebSocket clients)
743   if (g_websocket_client) {
744     log_debug("Destroying WebSocket client");
745     websocket_client_destroy(&g_websocket_client);
746   }
747   if (g_tcp_client) {
748     log_debug("Destroying TCP client");
749     tcp_client_destroy(&g_tcp_client);
750   }
751 
752   // CRITICAL: Terminate PortAudio device resources FIRST
753   log_debug("Terminating PortAudio device resources");
754   audio_terminate_portaudio_final();
755 
756   // Stop audio thread before destroying audio context to prevent use-after-free
757   // The audio worker thread may still be logging when we destroy the buffer
758   APP_CALLBACK_VOID(audio_stop_thread);
759 
760   // Stop and destroy audio (after PortAudio is terminated and audio thread is stopped)
761   if (audio_ctx) {
762     audio_stop_duplex(audio_ctx);
763     audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
764     SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
765     audio_ctx = NULL;
766   }
767 
768   // Destroy display
769   if (display) {
770     session_display_destroy(display);
771     display = NULL;
772   }
773 
774   // Destroy capture
775   if (capture) {
776     session_capture_destroy(capture);
777     capture = NULL;
778   }
779 
780   // Destroy stdin reader if active (stdin render mode)
781   if (g_stdin_reader) {
782     terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
783     g_stdin_reader = NULL;
784   }
785 
786   // Clean up probe source if still allocated (shouldn't happen, but be safe)
787   if (probe_source) {
788     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
789     probe_source = NULL;
790   }
791 
792   // Stop splash animation and enforce minimum display time (even on error path)
793   // But skip completely if shutting down - don't interact with splash at all during shutdown
794   // The animation thread will exit on its own when it detects shutdown_is_requested()
795   if (!APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
796     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_intro_done()");
797     splash_intro_done();
798     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_intro_done() returned");
799 
800     // Wait for animation thread to exit before cleanup
801     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_wait_for_animation()");
802     splash_wait_for_animation();
803     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_wait_for_animation() returned");
804   } else {
805     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Skipping all splash operations (shutdown in progress)");
806     // During shutdown, don't interact with splash - let animation thread exit naturally
807     // and don't wait for it (prevents blocking on signals)
808   }
809 
810   // Stop debug sync thread before destroying log buffer to prevent use-after-free
811   log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call debug_sync_cleanup_thread()");
812   debug_sync_cleanup_thread();
813   log_debug("[CLEANUP] debug_sync_cleanup_thread() returned");
814 
815   // Disable keepawake (re-allow OS to sleep)
816   log_debug("[CLEANUP] Disabling keepawake");
817   platform_disable_keepawake();
818   log_debug("[CLEANUP] keepawake disabled");
819 
820   // Write newline to separate final frame from prompt (if configured)
821   log_debug("About to check print_newline_on_tty_exit");
822   if (config->print_newline_on_tty_exit && terminal_is_stdout_tty()) {
823     log_debug("Writing newline");
824     const char newline = '\n';
825     (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, &newline, 1);
826   }
827 
828   log_set_terminal_output(false);
829 
830   return result;
831 }






References APP_CALLBACK_BOOL, APP_CALLBACK_VOID,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(), audio_destroy(),
    audio_init(), audio_should_enable_microphone(),
    audio_start_duplex(), audio_stop_duplex(),
    audio_stop_thread(), audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    session_capture_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::color_mode,
    session_display_config_t::custom_palette,
    debug_sync_cleanup_thread(),
    session_client_like_config::discovery,
    session_capture_config_t::enable_audio, ERROR_DISPLAY,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_USAGE,
    GET_OPTION, session_capture_config_t::initial_seek_timestamp,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_enabled,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_filled, log_debug,
    log_fatal, log_info, log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_warn,
    session_capture_config_t::loop,
    session_client_like_config::max_reconnect_attempts,
    audio_context_t::media_source, media_source_create(),
    media_source_destroy(), MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE,
    media_source_get_video_fps(), media_source_has_audio(),
    media_source_set_audio_context(), MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN,
    MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST, MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM,
    OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT, OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT,
    session_display_config_t::palette_type,
    session_capture_config_t::path, platform_disable_keepawake(),
    platform_enable_keepawake(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    platform_write_all(), audio_context_t::playback_buffer,
    audio_context_t::playback_only,
    session_client_like_config::print_newline_on_tty_exit,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_delay_ms,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_user_data,
    session_capture_config_t::resize_for_network,
    session_client_like_config::run_fn,
    session_client_like_config::run_user_data, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, session_capture_destroy(),
    session_capture_get_media_source(),
    session_capture_set_audio_context(), session_display_create(),
    session_display_destroy(),
    session_display_reset_first_frame(),
    session_display_set_global_context(),
    session_display_set_render_audio_source(),
    session_display_set_render_fps(),
    session_display_set_stdin_reader(),
    session_mirror_capture_create(),
    session_network_capture_create(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_capture_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_display_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_client_like_config::should_reconnect_callback,
    session_display_config_t::snapshot_mode, splash_intro_done(),
    splash_intro_start(), splash_restore_stderr(),
    splash_wait_for_animation(),
    session_capture_config_t::target_fps,
    session_client_like_config::tcp_client, tcp_client_destroy(),
    TERM_COLOR_AUTO, terminal_fd_reader_create(),
    terminal_fd_reader_destroy(), terminal_is_stdin_tty(),
    terminal_is_stdout_tty(), terminal_should_force_stderr(),
    time_elapsed_ns(), time_get_ns(),
    session_capture_config_t::type,
    session_client_like_config::websocket_client, and
    websocket_client_destroy().


Referenced by client_main(), discovery_main(), and
    mirror_main().






void
  session_client_like_set_websocket_client (websocket_client_t *
  client)


Set the WebSocket client for this session.




Called by connection_attempt_tcp() after successfully
    creating a WebSocket client to update the global state. This ensures that
    session_client_like_run() correctly detects network mode instead of
    mirror mode.




Parameters


client WebSocket client instance, or NULL to
  clear


Definition at line 66 of file client_like.c.


66                                                                           {
67   g_websocket_client = client;
68 }












Variable
  Documentation




bool(*)(void *)
  session_client_like_get_render_should_exit(void) (void )


Get the render loop should_exit callback set by
    session_client_like_run().




This is used by mode-specific run_fn callbacks to obtain the
    proper should_exit callback for passing to session_render_loop(). The
    callback checks both the global should_exit() flag and any
    mode-specific custom_should_exit condition.




Returns


Function pointer to the render should_exit callback, or
  NULL if session_client_like_run() has not been called yet.


Definition at line 253 of file client_like.h.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.3
    ascii-chat
